"Let us assume the opposite, Professor Nayef: if the Iranian regime had managed to assassinate Netanyahu, the defense minister, the head of intelligence, the head of Mossad — what would they say then? That we were defeated? When the scene reaches this level of political decay and political delirium, we should not chase the arguments of madmen. We are not going to sit and talk about self-evident truths with them, to convince the pro-Iranians or the Mullah regime that it has been defeated. I am not going to convince him because he is like a man afflicted with color blindness — he sees green as red, and no matter how hard you try to convince him that what he sees as green is actually red, he will not believe you."

https://t.co/32dwric9G6

tweets.text:
Around $2.10B worth of investor ownership in private funds and companies currently exists in transferable tokenized form.

And one product makes up almost half of that entire category.

The BCAP fund, managed by @blockchaincap and tokenized through @Securitize, is valued at around $971.6M, accounting for roughly 46% of the market.

It is one of the three largest assets in the space, alongside the Titan III Equity Token and Hera I Equity Token, both issued through @CtrlAltCo.

Together, those three products make up around 72% of the category’s total market value.

Pretty insane when you think about it, but this is still a very early-stage market.

And I think that shows the tokenized RWA narrative is still being carried by a few large issuances rather than broad institutional adoption.
tweets.full_text:

It is one of the three largest assets in the space, alongside the Titan III Equity Token and Hera I Equity Token, both issued through @CtrlAltCo.

Together, those three products make up around 72% of the category’s total market value.

Pretty insane when you think about it, but this is still a very early-stage market.

And I think that shows the tokenized RWA narrative is still being carried by a few large issuances rather than broad institutional adoption.

tweets.text:
This was the was the easiest, most kind and gentle bear market ever for crypto. That’s a fact. 

It had the smallest drawdown in its history, has been quite short. And no blowups or contagion risks. You could have traded other markets and made big gains that rivaled alts from 2021. even Nigerian ones, potato markets, ai etc.  everything went up. the downtrend was only isolated to crypto and respected simple trends

tweets.text:
it's beyond just fraud. 

it's clear-eyed industrialized plundering of the federal government by blue states. most of the wasted funds aren't even "fraudulent" – the programs are designed to shovel cash out the door. they are working as designed. 

effectively, blue states have created massive welfare programs under the guise of healthcare. most of these jobs are completely fake but with plausible deniability baked in. it's great for the blue states because they can pay off their racial client groups with fake make-work 0 effort jobs, and the federal government foots most of the bill. it allows them to depict their GDP stats in an overly rosy light and pretend that plenty of job growth is happening. of course, it's all government funded, and the jobs are producing no economic value, so it's strictly negative from a fiscal perspective. 

the leadership in these blue states knows that eventually these systems will grow too large and the states will default. but they expect that they will be bailed out by the feds at that point. and they probably will! because what monster would be against "healthcare"? so when you wonder why healthcare is 30% of the federal budget and 35% of CA's budget, understand that it's mainly being used as a covert government jobs program

tweets.text:
Cyclicals should be bought when they are unprofitable or trading at super high PEs; and they should be sold after big rallies when their PEs appear cheap. 

Commodity-type industries sometimes get priced as secular growth but a new generation of investors learn the same lesson.
